channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: The Tools to Ending HIV/AIDS Are Here, but US Cuts Damage Progress
item.description: The campaign to finally conquer the global HIV/AIDS crisis has run into a frustrating paradox: just as effective new treatments are promising the goal is within grasp, funding cuts by the Trump administration are pushing it further away. Since the epidemic erupted in the 1980s, rapidly spreading around the world, the US has been the ... Read more

item.title: Security Council LIVE: Spotlight on Syria as Guterres heads to Damascus
item.description: The Security Council is holding its monthly debate on Syria amid reports of an Israeli attack in occupied territory and discussions of a multi-national oil pipeline to counter the closure of the critical Strait of Hormuz – still the focus of the ongoing US-Iran war. The meeting follows UN independent investigators’ findings that security conditions remain fragile in the south as the UN chief heads to Damascus for his first visit to the country as Secretary-General.

item.title: DR Congo: Ebola outbreak still expanding, WHO sees signs of stabilization
item.description: In the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), UN health teams and partners are still racing to contain the rapidly expanding Ebola outbreak, despite ongoing insecurity in eastern areas where the virus has taken hold. As of 19 July, the DRC Government has recorded 2,423 confirmed cases, including 967 deaths and 469 recoveries.

item.title: UN refugee agency reports 144 migrants dead or missing off Mauritanian coast
item.description: The UN’s refugee agency (UNHCR) reports that 144 migrants attempting to reach Europe are dead or missing due to a series of “tragic maritime incidents” off the coast of Mauritania last week.

item.title: Global hunger declines again but progress remains uneven across regions
item.description: Global hunger declined for a third consecutive year in 2025 showing that progress is possible, but gains remain fragile and far too slow to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by the 2030 deadline.

item.title: ‘Business as usual is a business of death’: UN adopts road safety pledge over US objection
item.description: The UN General Assembly closed its first ever High-level Meeting on road safety on Tuesday by adopting a sweeping new declaration aimed at halving road deaths by 2030 – over the sole objection of the United States.

item.title: World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia
item.description: Independent UN human rights experts on Tuesday condemned the M23 armed group’s “reign of terror” over territory it controls in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), calling for an end to the Rwanda-backed group’s sexual torture and violence against civilians.

item.title: Israeli attacks on Gaza intensify amid surge of West Bank settler violence
item.description: Amid intensified deadly Israeli attacks in Gaza, the UN chief and top officials condemned the killings while a surge violence rocked the West Bank and aid agencies scrambled to help Palestinians.

item.title: Critical minerals: The hidden resources powering the clean energy future
item.description: Why are critical minerals suddenly at the centre of global politics?

item.title: Guterres calls for stepping up diplomatic efforts to end US-Iran conflict
item.description: The United Nations again called for restraint as war ramps up in the Middle East, underscoring the need for dialogue and an end to attacks on “vital infrastructure” across the region.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence and the Future of Humanity: Nobel Laureates Call for Global Safeguards
item.description: “I’m sorry, Dave. I’m afraid I can’t do that.” By bringing the supercomputer HAL 9000 to life in his 1968 film 2001: A Space Odyssey, American director Stanley Kubrick created one of the most powerful symbols of the fear of artificial intelligence (AI) escaping human control. Fifty-eight years later, that question is more relevant than […]

item.title: Global Value Chain Trade Reaches Historic Highs, Despite Push for Reshoring
item.description: Despite rising geopolitical tensions, trade restrictions and calls to bring production back to domestic shores, the global economy remains more interconnected than it may appear. Trade through global value chains (GVCs) reached historic levels in 2024, according to a new report from the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD). GVCs, the cross-border production networks […]

item.title: From the General Assembly to Harlem: Mandela’s Call to Action Endures
item.description: Sixty-seven minutes. That is how long volunteers spent serving lunch to people experiencing food insecurity at Refettorio Harlem in New York City last week – one for every year Nelson Mandela gave to public service. Diplomats stood alongside city officials and neighbourhood volunteers, dishing out plates rather than platitudes, in a small but pointed answer […]
